AF6JQZLKIXH6SCNOOQDOJRD2DLMQ.review_text: Great collection for a great price. I've only played 1-3 right now, but having finished them all, I enjoyed the experience. I have the two PSP games left and I expect those will be great too.
AF6JQZLKIXH6SCNOOQDOJRD2DLMQ.best_prediction_review: If you missed the older God of War games, this collection is a ton of content for the money. You’re getting 1–3 plus the two handheld entries, and I actually worked through them again which says enough about the value. Story-wise it’s the full Kratos revenge arc in one place, for better or worse.

AGEIJQLQ5D3MPYODEAMXGCUCIPPA.product_description: Product description Enter the world of Lars Ulrich, James Hetfield, Kirk Hammett and Robert Trujillo and experience the intensity and skill of one of the greatest bands of all time. Shred on the guitar controller, rip on the drum kit controller and wail on the mic to over 45 explosive hits from both Metallicas illustrious career and a variety of other legendary artists selected by the band, such as Alice In Chains, Bob Seger & The Silver Bullet Band, Foo Fighters, Lynyrd Skynyrd and Queen. Guitar Hero Metallica introduces an all-new Expert+ drum difficulty setting, featuring two bass drum kick pedals that offer an authentic Metallica experience with note-for-note tracking, and Metallifacts, which, after nailing a song, lets players enjoy a virtual concert filled with on-screen facts and trivia about the song being played. *DLC (Downloadable Content) may not be included and is not guaranteed to work* Amazon.com Metallica and Guitar Hero fans rejoice: Guitar Hero: Metallica has landed for PlayStation 3. Like Guitar Hero: Aerosmith before it, Guitar Hero: Metallica will focus on the career and songs of Metallica. With 28 Metallica songs and 21 other songs that influenced the band, fans will love playing guitars, drums, and singing vocals alongside Kirk Hammett, Lars Ulrich, James Hetfield, and Robert Trujillo. Follow the Career of Metallica and Play as Your Own Upstart Guitar Hero: Metallica is similar to Guitar Hero World Tour in that you play a full suite of instruments. When the game starts, you're the new band on the block with aspirations of opening for Metallica. The game goes back and forth between performances by Metallica and performances of songs that influenced Metallica by your band. The band was motion captured so you'll recognize their unique motions on screen and style of playing. As you progress in the game, you'll start to unlock bonus features such as videos of old Metallica performances, backstage photos, and "Metallifacts," which enables different trivia to show up while a song is playing, similar to VH1's Pop-Up Video. Metallica Recordings from Original Masters and More Via Download Guitar Hero: Metallica features 49 songs in total, 28 Metallica songs, and 21 songs that are favorites of the band and have influenced them. The Metallica songs are sourced from original masters so you'll have the best audio quality available. You'll also be able play downloadable content from their latest album, Death Magnetic. Game Difficulty Goes from One All The Way Up to Eleven Metallica exhibit technical prowess while performing, and this game goes from Beginner all the way to Expert Plus difficulty. Hardcore gamers will not be disappointed. You'll have to play guitars, drums, and sing, and in the "Expert Plus" difficulty, you'll have to plug in a second bass pedal for double bass pedal drum work. Real-Life Venues As you go on tour, you'll be playing venues small and large, and you might recognize some that you've been to. Famous venues that played a part in Metallica's career such as The Stone and the Hammersmith Odeon are included in the game and have been duplicated with great detail. Grab Your Friends and Play Together Multiplayer is part of what made Guitar Hero such a hit and it is no different here. Gather your friends and family, and jam Metallica tunes together. The game also features Metallica-specific power ups for competitive multiplayer. For example, "Fade to Black" will black out your opponent's notes, and "Trapped Under Ice" will freeze their whammy bar. Create Your Own Music Those familiar with Guitar Hero World Tour will welcome back the Music Studio, where you can create new music and then upload and share them through the GHTunes service. The Music Studio in Guitar Hero: Metallica gives you Metallica specific tones such as Hetfields' ESP Truckster guitar, as well as Metallica drum sounds. Songs made in Metallica and World Tour are compatible with each other through the GHTunes service.
AGEIJQLQ5D3MPYODEAMXGCUCIPPA.review_text: As far as band exclusive games this is the holy grail. I got it because a friend let me play it at his house and was surprise with the inclusion of a Mercyful Fate and that King Diamond is also in the game. I have played using the drums and that makes it double the fun to play.
AGEIJQLQ5D3MPYODEAMXGCUCIPPA.best_prediction_review: If you’re a Metallica fan, this is the band-focused Guitar Hero that actually feels worth it. The Metallica tracks hit hard and the audio mix is solid, and the motion-captured band stuff is the kind of fan-service that made us grin the first time it kicked in. Gameplay-wise the drums are where it shines, and Expert+ with the double pedal is no joke. Graphics are basically World Tour-level, so don’t expect a big visual upgrade.

AGEIJQLQ5D3MPYODEAMXGCUCIPPA.product_description: Product Description This fall, living room legends will rock any way they want in Guitar Hero 5. The entire set list is at players’ finger tips from the start, featuring genre-defining hits from some of the biggest rock artists and bands of all time, including The Rolling Stones, The White Stripes, Kings of Leon, Tom Petty, Bob Dylan, Johnny Cash, Santana and Vampire Weekend. Players take complete control as Guitar Hero 5 allows gamers to personalize and customize how they experience music by being able to play with any in-game instrument combination -- multiple vocalists, guitarists, bassists and drummers, in any game mode. With the game’s all-new Party Play Mode, getting the band back together has never been easier. Players can now jump in or drop out of gameplay seamlessly, without interrupting their jam session. Elevating the Guitar Hero series to new heights with unmatched social gameplay, an enhanced style, new in-game artists and more rock legends, Guitar Hero 5 features new surprises and challenges that will fire-up long-time fret board fanatics and create a new generation of enthusiasts. Amazon.com Featuring the best variety and biggest rock stars of today combined with classic rock anthems, Guitar Hero 5 cranks it up again for video game rockers. For the first time ever, players can customize the make-up of their band by rocking with any combination of instruments in-game: whether it be two guitars and two drums, or three guitars and a microphone, any combination is possible, allowing players to experience music their own way. .caption { font-family: Verdana, Helvetica neue, Arial, serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: bold; font-style: italic; } ul.indent { list-style: inside disc; text-indent: -15px; } table.callout { font-family: verdana; font-size: 11px; line-height: 1. 3em; } td.vgoverview { height: 125px; background: #9DC4D8 url(https://images-na.ssl-images-amazon.com/images/G/01/electronics/detail-page/callout-bg.png) repeat-x; border-left: 1px solid #999999; border-right: 1px solid #999999; padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px; padding-bottom: 10px; width: 250px; font-family: verdana; font-size: 12px; } New modes like RockFest. View larger . Play any combo of instruments. View larger . Rock with Shirley Manson of Garbage. View larger . And Carlos Santana. View larger . Classic Guitar Hero action. View larger . Brand new, innovative, easy-to-use gameplay modes like Party Play and RockFest put fun, competition and control at center stage as fans tailor the Guitar Hero experience to match their personal style and interests. For extended hours of entertainment, downloadable content from Guitar Hero World Tour will be compatible with the game and will automatically be updated to include all of the upgrades and enhancements of Guitar Hero 5 . Assemble Your Band and Rock Any Way You Want Guitar Hero 5 allows fans to play alone, as a full band or, for the first time, with any combination of multiple guitarists, bassists, drummers or vocalists, to customize their musical experience. Players now have the control to rock any way they want whether it is with two guitars and two drummers, four guitarists, or three guitarists and a vocalist, any combination is possible. This freedom is available in all modes of play: online, Party Play, Quickplay, RockFest and even Career. The Definitive Rock ‘n’ Roll Set List Experience furious finger fretting, intense drumming, and lyrics that will challenge even the best vocal chops with songs from some of the hottest bands of today to all-time favorite classic anthems including: Tom Petty, Kings of Leon, The White Stripes, Santana, Johnny Cash, The Rolling Stones and Bob Dylan. Featuring 85 master tracks, the breadth of rock-centric styles ensures that every Guitar Hero fan will find their favorite tracks and discover new ones. Join The Party It has never been easier to rip it up and rock out with friends than it is in Guitar Hero 5 . The brand new Party Play Mode puts players straight into the action with the press of a button and allows them to jump in or drop out, or switch difficulty level at any time without interrupting the jam session. Become the Best. Shred the Rest In Guitar Hero 5 players can test their skills against others with four-player local and up to eight-player online battles in the all-new competitive RockFest Mode. Within RockFest Mode, players can choose from various head-to-head gameplay types, including Momentum, Perfectionist, Elimination, Do-or-Die, Streakers, and Pro Face Off. Limitless Jams Guitar Hero 5 delivers hours of endless entertainment, as fans will be able to play Guitar Hero World Tour downloadable content in the game, experiencing all of the upgrades and new features in Guitar Hero 5 . AGEIJQLQ5D3MPYODEAMXGCUCIPPA.review_text: I was disappointed with some of the previous releases. Like the new features such as Party Play and Rockfest mode where you can play mini-games against your friends. Graphics are still pretty much the same with some new venues, but nothing to blow you out of your seat. I do like how one player can pause their instrument and the game continues for the other players and how you can switch instruments and difficulties at any moment. Everyone can also chose to play the same instrument instead of fighting who plays what.
AGEIJQLQ5D3MPYODEAMXGCUCIPPA.best_prediction_review:
If you’re buying Guitar Hero 5, the “it’s real” moment is the setlist and how painless it is to keep a room playing. The mix is legit (I still get a kick out of stuff like King Diamond/Mercyful Fate popping up), and drums are a blast.

What’s actually new vs older GH isn’t some huge overhaul, but Party Play is the keeper feature: people can jump in/out and even swap instruments/difficulty without killing the song, which saves a lot of living-room arguments. Just make sure you already have compatible instruments for your system.

AH4NQPPYEFNU2N75JPX2QD76WPWA.product_description: Collectable Spyro cable guy device holder - works with PlayStation and Xbox controllers and all SMARTPHONES. Includes 2M USB cable with adapters. Figures is 8" Tall.
AH4NQPPYEFNU2N75JPX2QD76WPWA.review_text: Perfect for any Spyro lover! He can hold my PS controller, phone or glasses! Very handy & I love having him around! This isn't just for the younger generations. So don't be worried about surprising someone older. If they like Spyro they will like this.
predicted_themes: Hardware Build Quality Comfort and Connectivity
AH4NQPPYEFNU2N75JPX2QD76WPWA.best_prediction_review: Spyro looks AWESOME on my desk and actually holds my PS5 controller steady (and my phone fits too). The 8” figure feels solid, not flimsy, and the included 2M cable/adapters is a nice bonus. Only gripe is the price can be a bit steep for a holder, so I’d grab it on sale. Great for fellow Spyro fans!

AH4NQPPYEFNU2N75JPX2QD76WPWA.product_description: Destiny Expansion II: House of Wolves - PS4 [Digital Code]
AH4NQPPYEFNU2N75JPX2QD76WPWA.review_text: It really aggravates me when people give a single star due to some issue other than if they are happy with the item. But in this case I don't know what else to do. IF YOU BUY THIS CODE when you attempt to enter it, you get a message telling you it will be at least Dec. 2014 before you can use the first one. And Jan-Feb 2015 before you can use the other one. I notified Amazon of this & they did give me a prompt refund. But their lack of updating the page so other buyers are aware has prompted this review. But I can say for Destiny itself, I'm sure it will be a great add on.....later this year & early next year. Once we repurchase it when it is usable.....I will update the stars.
AH4NQPPYEFNU2N75JPX2QD76WPWA.best_prediction_review:
House of Wolves was the Destiny add-on that finally got my group logging in on purpose again. Prison of Elders is just plain fun (we’d do “one more run” and suddenly it’s late), and Trials gave my competitive friends something to chase.

PSA before you buy this digital code: you MUST already own Destiny on PS4, and the code has to match your PlayStation account region. I’ve seen people grab the “wrong” region/edition stuff on accident and then you’re just stuck staring at a code you can’t use. If you’re buying it as a gift, double check that too.

If you’re still actively playing Destiny with friends, this one was 100% worth it for us.

AH4NQPPYEFNU2N75JPX2QD76WPWA.product_description: Product Description Step inside a visionary entertainment experience that synchronizes stunning graphics and innovative sound design to electrify your senses. In Child of Eden, the newest project from renowned game designer Tetsuya Mizuguchi, Microsoft Kinect enables you to use your whole body as the controller, with no physical barriers between the game world and you. The unique synergy of movement, audio, and visuals makes Child of Eden a groundbreaking total sensory experience that is literally unforgettable. From the Manufacturer O enredo da aventura coloca o jogador na missão de resgatar uma mulher, Limi, que está presa em Eden, um mundo virtual equivalente à internet no futuro O problema é que esse mundo está infectado por um vírus, sendo que durante sua jornada será necessário purificar todos os Archives ("arquivos" no lugar de fases) de Eden O jogador recebe três tipos de armas: o tiro comum, a metralhadora e o que os desenvolvedores chamam de "Happy Bomb", ou "Bomba Feliz" Além da opção de utilizar os controles comuns das plataformas, a versão para Xbox 360 oferece a possibilidade de aproveitar o periférico Kinect para amplificar a sensação de ser o regente de uma orquestra.
AH4NQPPYEFNU2N75JPX2QD76WPWA.review_text: This game is a game but it's also a total mind trip. I gave away my Playstation Move quite some time ago. But due to this game I will be buying another one just so the game is a bit more "trippy". We currently play with just a controller & it works fine.
AH4NQPPYEFNU2N75JPX2QD76WPWA.best_prediction_review: Child of Eden is a trippy, fun “zone out and play” game, but don’t buy it thinking you NEED Kinect. I tried both and the regular controller works great (and honestly felt less frustrating than waving at the sensor). Story is light, it’s more about getting into a rhythm and clearing stages. If you like weird artsy games, you’ll be happy.

AFZW6S6YZI7PRMGHXPQQIXPWNE6A.product_description: Bundle includes: -New Nintendo 3DS HW -Two Cover Plates (Blastoise & Charizard) -Pre-Installed games Pokemon Red Version and Pokemon Blue Version -Exclusive Home Menu theme AC Adapter sold separately. 3D Mode recommended for Ages 7+
AFZW6S6YZI7PRMGHXPQQIXPWNE6A.review_text: Very nice for the BASE PRICE! Not the scalper prices! Comes with the two games as advertised, console itself, and the two pairs of faceplates! There's also a few of those scannable cards with nintendo characters as well as two Red and Blue themes for the system HUD! Absolutely love it makes me a very happy Pokemon fan! It's the new nintendo 3ds too so the face plates can be removed and replaced with other faceplates out there like the fire emblem ones! It can also scan amiibos too!
predicted_themes: Hardware Build Quality Comfort and Connectivity
AFZW6S6YZI7PRMGHXPQQIXPWNE6A.best_prediction_review: As a Pokemon fan this New 3DS bundle is such a cool pickup. The Blastoise/Charizard plates look awesome in person and the system feels solid in hand, like it’ll hold up. Having Red and Blue already on there is the best part—turn it on and you’re playing, plus the exclusive Home Menu theme is a nice little extra. Price-wise it felt worth it to me because you’re getting the console + the plates + two classic games in one package.

AFZW6S6YZI7PRMGHXPQQIXPWNE6A.product_description: Ortz PS4 Vertical Stand with Cooling Fan [Keeps System Cool] Playstation Controller Charging Station with Dual Charger [NOT for PRO or Slim]
AFZW6S6YZI7PRMGHXPQQIXPWNE6A.review_text: Awesome Rig love the fact that it has the 3 USB ports! The Fan is also pretty good and I really like the fact that there's a switch to turn it off and on as well. Charging stations are pretty snug and easy to use without worry of breaking or dropping controllers.
predicted_themes: Hardware Build Quality Comfort and Connectivity
AFZW6S6YZI7PRMGHXPQQIXPWNE6A.best_prediction_review: Nice stand for the BASE PRICE. My original PS4 clicks in solid and doesn’t feel like it’ll tip over, and the controller docks are the best part for me since they line up easy and actually charge without fighting the ports. The fan is a small bonus but I mainly wanted the cleaner setup. Just note it’s NOT for Pro or Slim.

AFZW6S6YZI7PRMGHXPQQIXPWNE6A.product_description: StarCraft II: Heart of the Swarm Expansion Pack
AFZW6S6YZI7PRMGHXPQQIXPWNE6A.review_text: Super fun game. Love the story and the design of the game itself.
AFZW6S6YZI7PRMGHXPQQIXPWNE6A.best_prediction_review: Heart of the Swarm is a really fun StarCraft II add-on. The story/cinematics are awesome and it feels like a proper continuation, especially if you liked the Zerg focus.

AGAUNV3W5P33DEOOENKMDCWZYEQQ.product_description: Product Description Amazon Basics Xbox One Controller Thumb Grips - Pack of 2, Orange From the Manufacturer Amazon Basics
AGAUNV3W5P33DEOOENKMDCWZYEQQ.review_text: I was excited to give these a try especially the left/right stick extenders.  It was super easy to attach them to my Xbox One controller and at first it seemed to make play more comfortable.  The problem is that they don't stay in place.  The buttons that attach to the L/R controller started falling off after the first use and the ones that are supposed to stick to the flippers never really stuck at all.  I definitely wasn't impressed and wouldn't recommend them for purchase.
predicted_themes: Hardware Build Quality Comfort and Connectivity
AGAUNV3W5P33DEOOENKMDCWZYEQQ.best_prediction_review:
I bought these Amazon Basics thumb grips for my Xbox One controller because the sticks get slick once my hands start sweating, and I wanted a little more control without buying a whole new controller. When they stay on, the texture definitely helps in shooters and my thumbs don’t feel as beat up after a longer session.

The problem is the fit/hold. One grip has been fine, but the other works loose and will sometimes pop off mid-game, which is basically the worst time for it to happen. I re-seated it and tried to make sure it was on evenly, but it keeps happening. For me that’s enough to pass and try a different brand/size.

AGAUNV3W5P33DEOOENKMDCWZYEQQ.product_description: Witcher 3: Wild Hunt Complete Edition - Nintendo Switch
AGAUNV3W5P33DEOOENKMDCWZYEQQ.review_text: Seriously the best game I've ever played.  This edition comes with two bonus campaigns as well.  The story, the graphics and the play value are all outstanding.  I've put in probably hundreds of hours of play while completing all campaigns at least twice.  The great thing is that the story changes with decisions that you make so you essentially get a new story/game each time.  There is romance, suspense, adventure, magic and the fight against evil.  There are funny characters as well as very serious even frightening characters.  I can only think of one other game that has impressed me nearly this much and it was Fallout 4 but this is even better.  There are some adult situations involved in the story so this isn't for kids younger than teens.  If you are a gamer who really enjoys getting into the story, you will love this.
AGAUNV3W5P33DEOOENKMDCWZYEQQ.best_prediction_review:
I’ll be honest, I wasn’t sure The Witcher 3 on Switch would be anything more than a “neat idea,” but I’ve been really impressed.  The story is still top tier and the Complete Edition gives you a ridiculous amount of content for the money.  Geralt’s world feels alive, choices actually matter, and it’s not a game you burn through in a weekend.

That said, you do have to go in with realistic expectations.  The visuals are obviously dialed back compared to bigger consoles and the performance can get a little choppy in busy areas, but I haven’t had anything game-breaking.  Controls feel good handheld once you get used to the menus.

Content warning for parents: this is very mature (violence, language, sexual content).  If you want an epic RPG you can take anywhere, this is absolutely worth grabbing—just don’t overpay if the price spikes.

AGAUNV3W5P33DEOOENKMDCWZYEQQ.product_description: Set within a sinister plantation mansion in modern day rural America and taking place after the dramatic events of Resident Evil 6, players experience the terror directly from the first person perspective for the first time in the series. Embodying the iconic gameplay elements of exploration and tense atmosphere that first coined "survival horror" some twenty years ago, Resident Evil 7 biohazard delivers a disturbingly realistic experience that will define the next era in horror entertainment.
AGAUNV3W5P33DEOOENKMDCWZYEQQ.review_text: I am a massive RE fan.  The first RE game years ago was what got me started in gaming and I was so excited for this new game.  I pre-ordered it something like a year early.  With all that being said, I was a bit disappointed to say the least.  The game visually is appealing.  The graphics are creepy if sometimes a bit blurry.  There also seems to be a lot of repetition with imagery from the older games.  Nitpicky?  Maybe but after paying $60 for a game, I expect a lot.  The first few minutes of gameplay were really spooky and got my adrenaline pumping but after that, things slow down quite a bit.  It takes forever to clear the old house and get on to the next chapter of the game so I was thinking, "Well at least it'll be a good, long game if it took this long to get past part one."  Nope.  The rest of the game moves along pretty quickly.  The boss fights aren't anything terribly new or challenging.  My biggest issue was the glitches I got.  I had a "failure to save game" error at about 75% through the game and ended up having to uninstall and then reinstall the game in order to fix the problem.  Not fun after having spent time getting that far into the game and Capcom tech support was zero help with the issue.  I searched the net for hours trying to find someone who could give me a solution that didn't involve uninstalling to no avail.  Once I finally played through to the end, I wasn't happy with how short the game was.  Games like Fallout 4 or Deus Ex gave me days of play which is what I expect for a top level game.  Total play time on this for me would probably have been 7-8 hours if I hadn't had to restart completely.  And to make matters worse, Capcom is already trying to milk us for more money with a DLC only a week after the game launched.  I was just terribly frustrated with the whole thing.  If you are a parent, be advised that this game is rated M for a reason.  There is ample blood and gore (of course) along with some pretty scary visuals.  I wouldn't recommend for any kids under 13.
AGAUNV3W5P33DEOOENKMDCWZYEQQ.best_prediction_review:
Resident Evil 7 is the first Resident Evil in a long time that genuinely creeped me out again.  The first-person view was a smart move because it makes the Baker house feel uncomfortably real, and the sound design does a lot of the heavy lifting (you hear things before you see them, and you don’t want to).  Visually it’s grimy and detailed in a way that fits the setting, and the whole “missing people / backwoods mansion” story kept me pushing forward even when I wanted to turn it off for the night.

Content-wise, this is not something I’d recommend for kids or even younger teens.  It’s intense, nasty, and pretty relentless, with some disturbing imagery and body horror.  If you like Resident Evil mainly for the characters and big action, this is a different flavor, but if you miss the older survival-horror pacing where every hallway matters, it delivers.

From a technical standpoint my playthrough was mostly stable (no progress loss or anything like that), but I did run into a couple of little hiccups where textures/pop-in were noticeable and there were brief stutters in a few spots.  Nothing that broke the game for me, but it’s a horror game so any jank stands out.

The only reason I’m not raving more is the value question.  I enjoyed the campaign and the atmosphere is top-tier, but it’s not one of those huge time-sink games.  At full price it felt a bit steep compared to the amount of story you get, especially if you’re the type who measures value by Fallout/Assassin’s Creed-style hours.  I’d absolutely recommend it on a sale, and for horror fans it’s worth playing, just don’t go in expecting a massive game for the money.

AGAUNV3W5P33DEOOENKMDCWZYEQQ.review_text: Very impressive game!  This totally open world game give players a constant variety of missions and options in a beautifully imagined virtual San Francisco.  You can choose to play online or not.  I generally choose not going online.  You play as Marcus a young hacker who is working with a group called DeadSec to prevent crime and government corruption by hacking people's phones and computer systems.  You gain followers by successfully completing missions which gains you points toward upgrades of your hacking abilities.  Players can choose to use lethal or nonlethal force against hostile forces or to use strictly stealth.  Of the missions I've completed thus far, one was particularly stand out.  You are tasked with infiltrating a pseudo religion called New Dawn which has all the hallmarks of the Church of Scientology. You work to rescue a particular male celebrity who looks and sounds a lot like Tom Cruise.  I loved it.  I also love the variety of locations and vehicles.  You can drive anything:  cars, motorcycles, trucks even boats to get you around the city.  You choose which missions to complete and when to complete them.  There is really nothing about this game that I haven't enjoyed so far.  Don't know if I mentioned it but the graphics are amazing.  I highly recommend this game.  There is violence and language so please pay attention to the rating.  It's not for the younger kids.
AGAUNV3W5P33DEOOENKMDCWZYEQQ.best_prediction_review:
I picked up Watch Dogs 2 Deluxe on Xbox One because I wanted a story-driven open world with a setting that feels like an actual place, and it delivered. The Bay Area vibe is spot on—rooftops in San Francisco, the Oakland sections, the tech-campus satire—it all feels lived in, not just “generic city.”

The gameplay hook that made it click for me is how often hacking turns into your main “weapon.” One of my favorite moments was setting off a chain reaction during a chase—traffic lights, barriers, cameras—while Marcus is still trying to stay low-key. It’s the kind of mission chaos you can retell later, and it’s different from just shooting your way through everything.

Story-wise, I liked Marcus and DedSec a lot more than I expected. It’s got humor, but it also leans into the creepy side of surveillance and data mining in a way that feels relevant. Content-wise, it’s not little-kid friendly (language and mature themes), but I’d be fine with teens who play story games.

I barely touched the online side (just not my thing), and I still felt like I got my money’s worth from the campaign and open-world messing around alone.

AECFWGF2CPMGZXNQRDXWRUGZGX7Q.product_description: Experience the World of Warcraft! World of Warcraft is an online role-playing experience set in the award-winning Warcraft universe. AECFWGF2CPMGZXNQRDXWRUGZGX7Q.review_text:
WoW is absolutely the best computer game I have ever played. Not only is it a superior game to look at, play, experience. It also is hooked up to millions of other players via the internet. So, you are playing with people from around the world. It is fascinating, addictive, and fun. It is something that you will want to play all the time, so be careful, and don't say that I did not warn you.

The online component is nice because lots of players are always on and will help you with the game. It does take a number of hours to learn most things, but people are willing to help you out. The nice thing also is that you can play as often, or as little as you want. One small piece that is, once you get to lvl 60, or lvl 70 with the patch that is coming up, I don't know what else there would be to achieve. But, overall a great game.

JVD
AECFWGF2CPMGZXNQRDXWRUGZGX7Q.best_prediction_review:
If you somehow missed the WoW wave back in the day, this base game + Burning Crusade bundle really is a time machine. The world is massive, the vibe is still unmatched, and it’s dangerously easy to log in “for a bit” and realize you just lost 4 hours questing, crafting, and then jumping into a dungeon with total strangers who end up feeling like a team.

Real talk before you buy: this is not a standalone, play-offline RPG. You’re signing up for an online MMO experience, and you’ll need an active account and a decent connection to actually play. Also, it’s a commitment—expect the first 10–15 hours to be mostly learning systems and running errands before the game’s group content and travel options start to click.

Once it does, it’s one of the easiest games to turn into a “friends game.” Running dungeons together, role coordination, and the social atmosphere are still the magic. Just go in knowing what it demands on day one.

AEGSZAI3IAS76436UUHE6ZG2VFDQ.product_description: Specter has failed over and over again in his attempts at taking over the world, all because of a few bungling monkeys. To rectify his problem, he decides to form the Ape Escape Academy, where he, serving as principal, will train monkeys to follow his will. Join Specter and his friends at the Ape Escape Academy and see if you can be the best monkey you can be.
AEGSZAI3IAS76436UUHE6ZG2VFDQ.review_text: This is an excellent game for those long trips, rainy days, or just a to pass time every now and then. It has over 25 individual games that test your memory, reflexes, and perception in games such as: Mon Quixote where you protect yourself with a shield from flying arrows, to monkey karate, RoShambo where you shoot rock paper scissors out of a gun to attack another monkey, monkey foosball, etc. The game also has an academy mode where a teacher gives you several test games, and if you pass you will get a mark on a game of tic-tac-toe. The furhter you go into your "studies" the more challenging the games will get, and the more lines you will have to complete in order to pass.
AEGSZAI3IAS76436UUHE6ZG2VFDQ.best_prediction_review:
This isn’t a full Ape Escape-style adventure, so set expectations: it’s basically “monkey school” trials and mini-games with a goofy little setup, and the story is just enough to frame why you’re doing challenges.

What made it click for me is the loop: you run academy tests, earn marks, and that opens up more sets with harder variations, so it’s easy to play a few rounds, quit, and come back later without feeling lost. The mini-games are simple but not brain-dead, and the controls are the make-or-break—most of them feel responsive and fair, but a couple activities have that PS2-era “learn the timing” feel where difficulty comes from precision.

If you want something light that you can keep dipping into (not a deep plot), this is a great casual pick.
